overview: Asmo Food is a quadrabonus tempo deck in Modern that leverages cheap persist creatures and Asmoranomardicadaistinaculdacar to generate relentless board presence. Archon of Cruelty provides critical life total manipulation and value generation upon death. The deck aims to outlast opponents through recursive value and aggressive pressure across W/B/R/G mana bases.
win_conditions: Overwhelming board presence with recurried persist creatures
Life total manipulation and drain via Archon of Cruelty

keep_criteria: Hands with early persist creatures or Archon of Cruelty
Hands containing Asmoranomardicadaistinaculdacar with cheap recursion targets
Mana bases supporting W/B/R/G early turns
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including duals or fetches), a turn-1 land drop, and either a turn-2 Archon of Cruelty, a mana accelerator, or a Persist enabler.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early board presence or recursion targets
Hands with only high-cost cards and no persist creatures
Mana screws in the quadrabonus color scheme
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, hands with only single-color sources and no fixers, or hands lacking any interaction or early threats.

early_game: Deploy cheap persist creatures to establish early board presence
Prioritize mana development to support W/B/R/G casting costs
Hold up mana to protect key threats from early removal
Turn 1: Play a basic or dual land, cast a mana dork or artifact ramp if available.
Turn 2: Develop your mana base, drop Archon of Cruelty if ahead, or play a Persist creature to establish board presence.
four-color consistency is critical to hitting your key spells.
mid_game: Cast Asmoranomardicadaistinaculdacar to begin recurring persist creatures
Use Archon of Cruelty to swing life totals and generate value
Maintain board pressure while filtering draw step for recursion targets
Balance mana for casting threats versus holding up interaction.
Use Food artifacts for card advantage and life gain to stabilize against aggressive strategies.
Preserve Persist creatures by protecting them or maintaining recursion resources in hand and graveyard.
Commit Archon of Cruelty when you can protect it or have removal ready for opposing threats.
Hold back Asmo until you have a clear path to victory, sufficient protection, or the opponent is tapped out for a combo win.
late_game: Chain persist returns to create unblockable or massive board states
Leverage Archon of Cruelty deaths to drain opponent life totals
Close out games with Asmo's trample damage or life total manipulation
Asmoranomardicadaistinaculdacar is the primary win condition, often cast for free or reduced cost.
Archon of Cruelty combined with Persist recursion creates an unblockable or resilient threat that forces multiple answers.
Go all-in when you have board control, enough mana to protect Asmo, or when the opponent is tapped out for a combo win.
Commit to attacking with Archon and Persist creatures when you can trade favorably or force the opponent to use multiple answers.

key_cards.name: Asmoranomardicadaistinaculdacar
key_cards.role: Primary recursion engine and aggressive threat
key_cards.notes: Hold up red mana to activate or protect, then trigger death effect to return target 2-mana or less creatures like persist enablers.

key_cards.name: Archon of Cruelty
key_cards.role: Life total manipulation and value generator
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice or let it die to swing life totals by 6, then recur it with Asmo for repeated value.

key_cards.name: Persist
key_cards.role: Recursive creature mechanic enabling infinite value loops
key_cards.notes: Play creatures with this ability to return from graveyard with a -1/-1 counter, then recur them again with Asmo for board presence.

sequencing_tips: Hold up red mana before Asmo dies to ensure it can be cast or protected
Time Archon of Cruelty deaths to maximize life swing before opponent's turn
Prioritize returning persist creatures with Asmo to maintain board pressure
Use W/B/R/G mana efficiently to cast recursion targets before graveyard hate
Asmo's casting cost or commander tax can be mitigated with specific effects
Persist creatures should be protected or recurred efficiently to maximize value.
Watch for format-specific hate cards and adjust your mulligan strategy to avoid dead draws.
Practice timing your Asmo cast to avoid counter spells and blink effects.

matchups.advice: Use persist creatures to outvalue early aggression and hold up removal for key threats
Race with Asmo's trample and use Archon of Cruelty to survive board wipes
Trade efficiently and recur key threats to overwhelm their resource advantage

Use cantrips, split casts, or cast Asmo during the opponent's upkeep when they are less likely to have counterspells. Utilize protection spells, free-cast effects, or sacrifice outlets to bypass counters and establish board presence.

Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use artifact hate and board wipes to slow their acceleration. Prioritize life gain and removal to survive their early game while outvaluing them midgame.

Win the resource war. Prioritize graveyard hate to disrupt Persist recursion. Use targeted removal to eliminate key threats. Control the board with wipes and win with Asmo or superior card advantage. Track opponent's hand size and graveyard to anticipate their moves.

common_threats: Watch for combo pieces (Energy counters, Storm counters, Amulet activations) and blink triggers.
Note when opponents tap out for key spells
this is your window to attack, cast Asmo, or deploy a Persist enabler.
Track graveyard contents to anticipate recursion plays.
